news_items.headline: Treasury Yields Fall, Dollar Strengthens as Oil Prices Slip
news_items.summary: Treasury yields cool down a little while the dollar rises as U.S.-Iran talks inch forward. Crude prices slip, as the U.S. cleared the way for Iran to sell oil in dollars, including to U.S. buyers, as part of the negotiations to restore shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

news_items.headline: Treasury Yields, Dollar Rise as U.S.-Iran Talks Stumble Along
news_items.summary: Treasury yields and the dollar rise amid tense negotiations in the Middle East. Oil prices decline as mediators say U.S.-Iran talks will continue this week, following a rocky start. The long disruption in global oil supplies, however, keeps inflation fears elevated.

news_items.headline: Bond Market Gives Its Feedback on the Fed
news_items.summary: The loudest feedback on Kevin Warsh's first press conference as Fed chairman came from the bond market. Just look at the Treasury yield curve. U.S. debt maturing in 10-years now yields only 0.7663 percentage points more than debt maturing in 2 years.

news_items.headline: Bonds Like the Fed's Hawkish Tilt
news_items.summary: Bonds are in the driver's seat as Kevin Warsh chairs his first Fed meeting. Look at the yield or interest rate on the 2-year note, which is up strongly by 0.097 percentage points. Higher yields on a shorter-dated note suggest the bond market is readying to see a rate hike by the Fed sometime this year.

news_items.headline: The Stock Market Is Waiting on the Fed
news_items.summary: The stock market was in a holding pattern ahead of Kevin Warsh’s first Federal Open Market Committee meeting press conference. The Dow was up 215 points, or 0.4%, and on pace for a closing high. May retail sales crushed expectations with 0.9% growth, but the FOMC meeting was all investors could think about.
